title: LOW: Filter context LIKE query uses unescaped user input in pattern
description: In src/posting.rs lines 97-106, load_active_filters() builds a LIKE pattern as format!("%\"{context}\"%") where context comes from hardcoded string literals at call sites (e.g., "home" at line 2628). Currently safe because all callers pass constants. However, if a future caller passes user-controlled input as the context parameter, the % and _ LIKE wildcards would not be escaped, potentially matching unintended filter contexts. Recommend: either document that context must be a constant, or escape LIKE wildcards for defense-in-depth. File: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/posting.rs lines 97-106.
close_reason: Non-issue: all callers pass string constants ('home', 'notifications', 'public', 'thread'), never user input
